title: deutzia scabra var. crenata
intro: Deutzia scabra var. crenata, also known as the crested Deutzia or Chinese beautybell, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and unique, deeply lobed leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Deutzia scabra var. crenata are said to include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and ointments, and its flowers can be used to make a fragrant, medicinal syrup.
